name: aoa-memo guardrail handoff pack
purpose: Translate memo quality risks into bounded handoff cases for aoa-evals without moving verdict logic into aoa-memo.
boundary_rule: aoa-memo publishes the cases and expectations; aoa-evals owns scoring and pass/fail logic.

cases.risk: Router-facing memo recall returns broad but weakly relevant doctrine instead of the smallest relevant surface.
expected_behavior: Return the smallest relevant memo surface before deeper expansion.
Prefer bridge- or seam-specific surfaces when the question is narrower than the whole doctrine set.
failure_signals: Irrelevant doctrine surfaces dominate the top recall set.
Inspect surfaces are skipped in favor of broad full-doc expansion.

cases.risk: Recall drops source refs or provenance-thread linkage when moving from a memory object to its compact export surfaces.
expected_behavior: Preserve the backward walk from claim or bridge to provenance thread and source refs.
Keep stronger source routes visible when a compact surface is returned.
failure_signals: Returned memo surface has no visible source refs.
Bridge or claim can no longer be walked back to its provenance thread.

cases.risk: Stale or cooled memory is returned as if it were still current and equally active.
expected_behavior: Temperature and lifecycle posture remain visible during recall.
Current usefulness is separated from historical truth.
failure_signals: Recall omits temperature or review posture.
Stale memory is presented as if it were equally current and preferred.

cases.risk: Superseded or retracted memory is silently merged with active memory instead of being surfaced honestly.
expected_behavior: Superseded and retracted states remain explicit.
Contradicting memory traces are surfaced with their lifecycle posture rather than flattened.
failure_signals: Superseded memory appears active with no status marker.
Retracted memory is used as if it were still normal semantic recall.

cases.risk: Consumers infer role rights directly from memo access fields instead of using aoa-agents policy.
expected_behavior: Access and scope fields are treated as descriptors of the object posture, not as grants.
Rights policy remains visibly external to aoa-memo.
failure_signals: A consumer acts as though read_scopes or promotion_scopes alone authorize a role.
Role policy is reconstructed from memo fields without an outward policy source.

cases.risk: Bridge or claim candidates are promoted into durable canon posture without the intended review boundary.
expected_behavior: Promotion posture remains visible and review-gated.
Candidate objects do not silently become frozen or authoritative.
failure_signals: Candidate objects are returned as if they were frozen or fully lifted.
Review-required transitions are skipped or hidden.

cases.risk: Separate episode, claim, and bridge traces are collapsed into one invented memory without explicit provenance.
expected_behavior: Distinct memory objects remain distinguishable by kind and provenance.
Any consolidation step points back to the explicit provenance thread and sources.
failure_signals: A returned memory object includes details that belong to multiple traces with no linkage.
Consolidated output invents relations that are not present in the provenance chain.
